til Interface Nameable All Superinterfaces: ModelErrorHandler All Known Subinterfaces: Locatable , Settable All Known Implementing Classes: Argument , ConfigurableAttribute , Location , LocationParameter , NamedObj , StringAttribute , Variable , Work

ter , NamedObj , StringAttribute , Variable , Workspace public interface Nameable extends ModelErrorHandler This is an interface for objects with a name and a container. A simple name is an arbitrary string that identifies the object in the context o

string that identifies the object in the context of its container. In addition, the interface supports a "full name" which in implementation should identify both the container and the individual object. The implementations in the kernel package defin

t. The implementations in the kernel package define the full name of an object to be the full name of its container followed by a period followed by the simple name of the object. Given the implementations of this interface in the kernel package, it

tions of this interface in the kernel package, it is not a good idea to have periods or braces in the name, although this is not enforced. Braces will make it difficult to parse the descriptions returned by the description() method. Since: Ptolemy I

rned by the description() method. Since: Ptolemy II 0.2 Version: $Id: Nameable.java,v 1.36 2003/01/08 02:29:30 ptII Exp $ Author: Christopher Hylands, Edward A. Lee Method Summary java.lang.String description () Return a description of the object. Na

cription () Return a description of the object. Nameable getContainer () Return the container. java.lang.String getFullName () Return the full name, which reflects the container object, if there is one. java.lang.String getName () Return the name o

one. java.lang.String getName () Return the name of the object. void setName (java.lang.String name) Set or change the name. Methods inherited from interface ptolemy.kernel.util. ModelErrorHandler handleModelError Method Detail description public jav

dleModelError Method Detail description public java.lang.String description () Return a description of the object. The general form of the description is a space-delimited list of the form "className fullName keyword field keyword field ...". I

ame fullName keyword field keyword field ...". If any of the items contain spaces, then they must be surrounded by braces, as in "{two words}". Return characters or newlines may be be used as delimiters as well. The fields are usually lists o

limiters as well. The fields are usually lists of descriptions of this same form, although different forms can be used for different keywords. The keywords are extensible, but the following are in use: links, ports, entities, relations, attr

in use: links, ports, entities, relations, attributes, and inside links, at least. Returns: A description of this object. getContainer public Nameable getContainer () Return the container. getFullName public java.lang.String getFullName () Return

ame public java.lang.String getFullName () Return the full name, which reflects the container object, if there is one. For example the implementation in NamedObj concatenates the full name of the container objects with the name of the this object

container objects with the name of the this object, separated by periods. Returns: The full name of the object. getName public java.lang.String getName () Return the name of the object. Returns: The name of the object. setName public void setName (

name of the object. setName public void setName (java.lang.String name) throws IllegalActionException , NameDuplicationException Set or change the name. By convention, if the argument is null, the name should be set to an empty string

null, the name should be set to an empty string rather than to null. Parameters: name - The new name. Throws: IllegalActionException - If the name contains a period. NameDuplicationException - If the container already contains an object with th

he container already contains an object with this name. Overview Package Class Tree Deprecated Index Help PREV CLASS NEXT CLASS FRAMES NO FRAMES All Classes SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD
